text: The physician is seated at the console, controlling the DaVinci robot to perform surgical procedures.
The surgeon demonstrates how the robotic arm remains stationary while the attached trocar inside the patient allows for movement around it.
The video shows the actual arm clutch of the surgical robot. Pressing the arm clutch button allows the surgeon to reposition the robotic arm as needed.
The demonstrator manipulates the robotic arm by pushing it down, adjusting its position to prevent the arms from hitting each other.
The demonstrator shows how the entire robotic arm system can be manually repositioned as needed, highlighting a feature not available on other systems.
The robotic trocars, equipped with etchings and marks, are positioned on the patient's abdomen, allowing the robot to read and align accurately. The robotic system is prepared for docking.
The video shows a close-up view of a robotic surgical instrument, specifically a metembalm or scissor, highlighting its design and functional area.
The robotic arm positions the trocar facing towards the gallbladder, indicating the anatomical area where the surgical procedure will be performed.
